back_translation: For the law is only a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the true picture of those things themselves; therefore, by the same sacrifices that are continually offered every year, the law is never able to make those who draw near complete.
If the law could do that, would those offerings of sacrifices not have stopped? Because if the worshipers had been cleansed once for all, they would no longer have any conscience of sin.
But on the contrary, those sacrifices are an annual reminder of sins.
For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and of goats can remove sins.
Therefore, when Christ came into the world, He spoke: 'Sacrifice and offering You did not desire, but a body You prepared for Me.
In burnt offerings and sin-atonement offerings You took no pleasure.
Then I said, "Behold, I have come — in the scroll of the book it is written about Me — O God, in order to do Your will."'

In the part above, where He said, 'Sacrifices, offerings, burnt offerings, and sin-atonement offerings, You did not desire and did not take pleasure in' — although those things were offered according to the law —
then He said, 'Behold, I have come, in order to do Your will.' He abolishes the first in order to establish the second.
By that will, we have received sanctification through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
And on one side, every priest stands performing duties every day, and offering the same kind of sacrifices repeatedly, which can never remove sins at all.
But on the other side, this priest, when He had offered only one sacrifice for sins, then He sat at the right hand of the hand of God forever.
Since that time, He waits until His enemies are made to be a footstool for His feet.
For by a single offering, He has made forever-perfect those whom He is in the process of sanctifying.
And the Holy Spirit also testifies to us about this matter; because after He had spoken,
'This is the covenant that I will make with them, after those days, the Lord declares — namely, I will put My laws into their hearts, and I will inscribe those laws into their minds,'
then He spoke additionally, 'Their sins and their transgressions, I will remember no more.'
And wherever there is forgiveness of these sins, an offering for sin is no longer needed.
Therefore, brothers and sisters, since we have boldness to enter into the Most Holy Place by the blood of Jesus —
the new and living way which He opened for us through the curtain — that is, His flesh —
and since we have a great priest who watches over the family of God —
let us draw near with a sincere heart, and with full faith, with hearts that have received sprinkling to cleanse from an evil conscience, and with bodies that have received washing with pure water.
Let us hold firmly to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the One who promised is faithful.
And let us consider one another, in order to spur to love and good deeds.
Do not abandon the gathering together, as some have done; but let us encourage one another, and the more so when you see that day approaching.
For if we deliberately go on sinning, after we have received the knowledge of the truth, no offering for sins remains anymore;
but only a fearful expectation of judgment, and of fire of fury that is about to consume those who are adversaries.
Anyone who rejected the law of Moses had to die without mercy, on the testimony of two or three persons.

How much heavier punishment do you think will be deserved by the one who trampled on the Son of God, who considered the blood of the covenant which sanctified him to be common, and who insulted the Spirit of grace?
For we know the One who said, 'Vengeance is Mine; I will repay,' and again, 'The Lord will judge His people.'
It is a fearful thing to fall into the hand of the living God.
But let those of you remember the early days, when after you had received the light, you endured a great struggle in the face of sufferings.
On one side, you were made into a public spectacle for ridicule, with mockings and afflictions; on the other side, you became fellow-companions of those who were so treated.
For you sympathized with those who were imprisoned, and you joyfully accepted the seizure of your possessions, knowing that you yourselves have a wealth that is better and that endures forever.
Therefore, do not throw away your boldness; for that boldness has a great reward.
For you need to have endurance, so that, when you have done the will of God, you may receive what He has promised.
For 'Yet a very little while longer, the One who is to come will come, and He will not delay.
My righteous one will live by faith; and if he shrinks back, My soul will take no pleasure in him.'
But we ourselves are not those who shrink back into destruction; rather, we are those who have faith in order to preserve our souls.
